finding: SILENT_IGNORE_VERDICT_STANDS — case materially strengthened since 2026-04-20 verdict set
summary: ISS-1281 asks the analyst to reconfirm the PRED-105 cucumber-growth verdict now that the dome has been silent for ~94h / 3.92d past the 2026-04-18 window closure. The analyst already set our_verdict=silent_ignore and logged FAIL-009 on 2026-04-20 (62.5h post-closure). The empirical record has only strengthened since: predictions.html has been byte-identical across 5 consecutive polls (md5=885cd58727130018764b14355b0aa785, 152,936 bytes), the last manual commit from Nhughes09 (a57e731) is now 115.0h / 4.79d old, 12 new commits during the latest poll window were 100% automated, and the PRED-105 tile text still reads "PENDING — PROSPECTIVE" with no PASS/FAIL/REFINED label or any goalpost-move to a widened window. No new WIN-070 denominator movement either. Recommendation: CLOSE ISS-1281, and optionally PATCH the PRED-105 verdict_notes to reflect the fresher timing (the verdict itself does not change).
kernel_of_truth: PRED-105 sits adjacent to a real bioelectromagnetics literature — weak EM fields have documented (though small, typically <5%, replication-troubled) effects on plant growth and seed germination, copper-wound coils are a recognizable laboratory form factor, and 46°N is inside the temperate cucumber-growing belt. The claim is not pure fabrication. It fails on (a) internal arithmetic: dome states sin θ = H₀/R_eq ⇒ 25.25° (not 46°); the value 46.20° is 2·arctan(H₀/R_eq) instead, so the angle is either retrofitted or the formula is misstated; (b) V50.6 consistency: H is not constant, H(R_eq) ≈ 837 km under the V50.6 exponential, so the fitting H₀=constant is incompatible with the dome’s own headline profile; (c) protocol vacuum: no N, randomization, blinding, statistical test, or growth metric; (d) silent-ignore: window closed, dome silent. The silent-ignore pattern confirms what the internal failure predicted — the prediction was not engineered to pay off or fail cleanly.

proposed_verdict_notes_patch.before: Window closed 2026-04-18 (14-day window). As of 2026-04-20T14:29Z (~62.5h post-closure), dome still labels PRED-105 PENDING-PROSPECTIVE with no PASS/FAIL/REFINED. Silent ignore logged in uncounted-failures.json as FAIL-009. Internal arithmetic failure noted: sin⁻¹(H₀/R_eq)=25°, not 46° as claimed.
proposed_verdict_notes_patch.after: Window closed 2026-04-18 (14-day window). As of poll 2026-04-21T22:07Z (~94h / 3.92d post-closure), dome still labels PRED-105 PENDING-PROSPECTIVE with no PASS/FAIL/REFINED. predictions.html byte-identical across 5 consecutive polls (md5 885cd58727130018764b14355b0aa785, 152,936 bytes); last manual commit a57e731 is 4.42d old. Silent-ignore now across 6 consecutive polls. Logged in uncounted-failures.json as FAIL-009. Internal arithmetic failure: sin⁻¹(H₀/R_eq)=25°, not 46°; dome H(r) profile also forbids any constant-H derivation at r=R_eq under V50.6.

fields_to_refresh.outcome: Window closed 2026-04-18. Dome site still shows PENDING-PROSPECTIVE ~94h (3.92d) later with no PASS/FAIL/REFINED label. Silent-ignore pattern: no verdict issued, no denominator change, no goalpost-move, no new WIN, no manual commit from Nhughes09 in 4.42 days. Silent-ignore now across 6 consecutive polls; predictions.html byte-identical across the most recent 5 polls (md5 885cd58727130018764b14355b0aa785).

proposed_failure_record_patch.rationale: Additive staleness refresh — does not change the silent_ignore classification or any claim about the dome, only updates the timestamps/durations so the record is self-consistent with the current poll evidence. Keep immutable fields (added_at, reconciliation_note) unchanged.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome author could still post a PASS/FAIL retroactively and break our silent-ignore classification.
anticipated_objections.response: The operational definition of silent-ignore already accounts for this. If a retroactive label appears on predictions.html the poller will detect the byte-level change, the decider will re-assign PRED-105, and our verdict will update. Silent-ignore is a snapshot of behavior during the window and for the immediate period after closure — the fact that the dome went 94h+ without acknowledging window expiry is itself the data, not invalidated by later retro-labeling, which would itself be a distinct noteworthy event.

anticipated_objections.objection: A 14-day cucumber-growth experiment genuinely can take longer than 14 days to resolve — the silence may be procedural, not strategic.
anticipated_objections.response: The prediction text is unambiguous: "14 days vs. control" is the stated comparison window and test_window_closes is 2026-04-18. If the author intended a longer observation period he authored the wrong test_window. Window extension after closure is itself a goalpost-move. The site has not posted any such extension — it has simply gone silent.

anticipated_objections.objection: PRED-105 is one of 10 prospective predictions; a single silent-ignore does not demonstrate a pattern.
anticipated_objections.response: Correct — this finding is scoped to PRED-105 alone. Broader patterns are tracked in uncounted-failures.json and the prospective-prediction roll-up in the walkthrough. The narrow claim here is that THIS prediction expired in silence under conditions (4.42d since any human commit, 5 byte-identical polls, 94h since window close) that rule out the "still drafting the outcome" charitable reading.

analyst_notes: No new EXP body is needed in sections.json or wins.json for this issue. The substantive analysis of PRED-105 is already present in data/predictions.json detail_reasoning (kernel, arithmetic failure, protocol gaps, biology plausibility, classification). This file is a closure finding for ISS-1281 plus two additive patches for staleness refresh. Not newsworthy on its own — the silent-ignore record is one data point in a broader prospective-prediction trend that the walkthrough already surfaces.
